{{park |name = Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Sone Kyuryo Park] * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum]

The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|12=draft}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|12=draft}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|12=draft}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Sone Kyuryo Park] * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum]

=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Sone Kyuryo Park] * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum]

revision.text:
{{park |name = Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|12=draft|12=draft}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|12=draft|12=draft}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Sone Kyuryo Park] * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum]

=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.

{{park |name = Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|12=draft|12=draft}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|12=draft|12=draft}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.

The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. ==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)

=== Other Facilities ===

Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.

=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.

{{park |name = Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|12=draft|12=draft}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|12=draft|12=draft}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.

The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. ==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)

=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft|12=draft}}.

Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.

=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.

{{park |name = Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|12=draft|12=draft}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|12=draft|12=draft}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.

==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)

=== Other Facilities ===

=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.

{{park |name = Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|12=draft|12=draft}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|12=draft|12=draft}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.

==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)

=== Other Facilities ===

Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.

=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.

{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==

{{park |name = Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|12=draft|12=draft}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|12=draft|12=draft}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.

Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==

{{park |name = Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|12=draft|12=draft}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|12=draft|12=draft}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft|12=draft}}.

The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|12=draft|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Access ==

Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.

[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]

Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|12=draft|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Sone Kyuryo Park] * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum]

{{park |name = Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|12=draft|12=draft}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|12=draft|12=draft}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]

Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.

== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|12=draft}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|12=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|12=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Sone Kyuryo Park] * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum]

[[sn:Kai Fudoki Hill]] [[zh:山梨県曽根丘陵公園]] [[ja:%E6%9B%BD%E6%A0%B9%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92]] [[sn:ai Fudoki Hill]] [[en:Kai Fudoki Hill]]
