{{park |name = Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]

The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)

revision.comment: 47 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:曽根丘陵公園 up to rev 98102595
revision.text:
{{公園 |名称 = 山梨県曽根丘陵公園 |画像 = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |分類 = [[都市公園]]（広域公園） |面積=40.4 ha |都市=[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]下向山1271 |位置情報={{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |開園=[[1986年]] |運営者=山梨県（[[指定管理者]]：富士観光開発・富士グリーンテック）<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html 指定管理者の導入施設一覧について]</ref> |事務所=曽根丘陵公園管理事務所 |事務所所在地=山梨県甲府市下向山町1271 |公式サイト=[http://www.sonekyuryo.jp 公式ホームページ] }} '''山梨県曽根丘陵公園'''（やまなしけんそねきゅうりょうこうえん）は[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]下向山町にある県立の[[都市公園]]<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html 山梨県都市公園条例]</ref>。'''甲斐風土記の丘'''（かいふどきのおか）の愛称がある。
==概要== [[曽根丘陵]]は[[甲府盆地]]の南西縁、[[笛吹川]]左岸の[[笛吹市]]境川地域から[[市川三郷町]]にかけての広がる9キロにわたる丘陵地域で、旧石器時代からの遺跡が数多く分布している。特に甲府市南部の東山地域は弥生後期の[[方形周溝墓]]群がある[[上の平遺跡]]や、[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]や大丸山古墳を中心とする前期[[古墳]]群が密集する地域で、銚子塚古墳の史跡整備に伴い公園が建設された。園内には遺跡や古墳群のほかに[[山梨県立考古博物館]]や研修センター、広場などがある。
==施設一覧== [[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|甲斐銚子塚古墳（2010年6月撮影）]]
=== 東山古墳群 === 曽根丘陵一帯にある16基の東山古墳群のうち、園内には県内最大の[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]（[[前方後円墳]]）と[[丸山塚古墳]]（[[円墳]]）がある。どちらも[[ハイキング]]コースの一部として整備されており、登ることが可能である。 {{節スタブ}} === 山梨県立考古博物館 === '''山梨県立考古博物館'''（やまなしけんりつこうこはくぶつかん）は曽根丘陵公園内にある[[博物館]]。[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）に開館<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author=山梨県教育委員会考古博物館 |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=考古博物館の紹介 |work=山梨県ホームページ |publisher=山梨県 |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref>。歴代館長に[[磯貝正義]]、[[大塚初重]]、[[渡辺誠 (建築家)|渡辺誠]]らがいる。
展示室は常設展示室・特別展示室の2つ。企画展示室はなく、年1回の秋期企画展に際しては常設展示の移動を行う。
常設展示は[[旧石器時代]]の[[ナイフ形石器]]をはじめ、[[縄文時代]]では国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている[[一の沢遺跡]]の出土品や[[殿林遺跡]]出土土器をはじめ[[有孔鍔付土器]]、[[人面装飾付土器]]、[[埋甕]]など各種土器群、[[ヒスイ]]の[[装身具]]や[[金生遺跡]]の[[配石遺構]]のジオラマなど。[[弥生時代]]では[[農耕具]]や[[弥生土器]]、[[古墳時代]]では[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]や[[大丸山古墳]]から出土した[[青銅鏡]]や[[短甲]]の[[複製品]]のほか実物資料では武具や近年出土した木製品、[[土師器]]・[[須恵器]]などが展示され、[[竪穴]]・横穴の[[石室]]構造が可動式の模型で展示されている。
そのほか、古代の[[墨書土器]]や[[甲府城]]から出土した輪宝、[[鰍沢河岸]]跡の出土品などが展示されている。
その他多目的室や学習・閲覧コーナー、[[山梨県埋蔵文化財センター]]の発掘調査報告書や研究紀要、博物館のグッズなどを販売している[[ミュージアムショップ]]が設置されている。初代館長の磯貝正義以来、[[考古学]]関係の文献を数多く収集し、「大塚文庫」として集成されている。
==== 特別展 ==== * 第一回 土偶-千の女神が語る縄文時代の祈りとくらし- * 第二回 縄文時代の酒造具-有孔鍔付土器- * 第三回 山梨の中世陶磁-やきものと甲斐人のくらし- * 第四回 古代甲斐国と畿内王権 * 第五回 古代官道と甲斐の文化 * 第六回 古代の装身具ー美と祈りの技術ー * 第七回 一粒の籾-弥生農耕の風景- * 第八回 古墳時代が聞こえる * 第九回 縄文土器 その心象世界 * 第十回 天下人の時代 * 第十一回 山梨の経塚 * 第十二回 古墳時代の甲冑 * 第十三回 黄金の都シカン発掘展 * 第十四回 ネアンデルタール人の復活展 * 第十五回 韓国・忠清北道の古代文化展 * 第十六回 遥かなるエジプト展-古代人の生活を探る- * 第十七回 縄文の旅-三内丸山から北陸、そして山梨へ- * 第十八回 中国四川省古代文物展 * 第十九回 古墳時代の金工品-黄金の輝き- * 第二十回 技と美の誕生-名宝でつづる縄文文化- * 第二十一回 遥かなるイスタンブール 大トルコ展-文明と美術- * 第二十二回 縄文の女神-人面装飾付土器の世界- * 第二十三回 縄文時代のくらし 山の民と海の民 * 第二十四回 甲府盆地から見たヤマト-甲斐銚子塚古墳の背景- * 第二十五回 世界遺産 ナスカ展 * 第二十六回 埋められた財宝ー大型装飾土器、銅鐸、そして埋蔵金ー * 第二十七回 卑弥呼時代の黄泉世界-上の平方形周溝墓群発掘30周年- * 第二十八回 発掘された女性の系譜-女性・子ども・家族- * 第二十九回 縄文土器名宝展-縄文芸術の到達点- * 第三十回 インカ帝国展-マチュピチュ「発見」100年- * 第三十一回 食いしんぼうの縄文人-いざとなったら縄文食、日本の食と心の源流- * 第三十二回 掘り起こされた音の形-まつりと音具の世界- * 第三十三回 縄文の美-世界に誇る山梨の縄文- * 第三十四回 よみがえる武士の魂-鎌倉・平泉と甲斐源氏の武家文化-<ref>『Museum Info 2016』</ref> * 第三十五回 ひつぎのヒミツ-棺から読み解く古墳時代-
=== 研修センター === '''風土記の丘研修センター'''（ふどきのおかけんしゅうせんたー）は曽根丘陵公園内にある山梨県立の研修所。[[1989年]]開館。
[[縄文土器]]作りや石器時代の火起こし体験などが体験できるほか、各種講演会場としても使用されている。 {{節スタブ}} === その他 === 園内には展望広場や[[バーベキュー|バーベキュー場]]、[[日本庭園]]、[[テニスコート]]などが設置されている。
==関連項目== *[[日本の歴史公園100選]]
==交通アクセス== *[[甲府駅]]（[[甲府駅バスターミナル]]）3番のりばより **[[山梨交通]]75系統「豊富」行きに乗車、「県立考古博物館」または「銚子塚古墳入口」下車。 *[[新宿駅]]（[[バスタ新宿]]）より **[[中央高速バス]] 新宿 - 甲府線(甲府南経由)に乗車、「中道」下車徒歩5分。 *[[中央自動車道]][[甲府南インターチェンジ]]より車すぐ。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
==外部リンク== {{commonscat|Sone kyuryo park}} *[http://www.sonekyuryo.jp 曽根丘陵公園] *[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html 山梨県立考古博物館]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[曽根丘陵公園]] to [[Kai Fudoki Hill]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{公園 |名称 = 山梨県曽根丘陵公園 |画像 = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |分類 = [[都市公園]]（広域公園） |面積=40.4 ha |都市=[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]下向山1271 |位置情報={{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |開園=[[1986年]] |運営者=山梨県（[[指定管理者]]：富士観光開発・富士グリーンテック）<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html 指定管理者の導入施設一覧について]</ref> |事務所=曽根丘陵公園管理事務所 |事務所所在地=山梨県甲府市下向山町1271 |公式サイト=[http://www.sonekyuryo.jp 公式ホームページ] }} '''山梨県曽根丘陵公園'''（やまなしけんそねきゅうりょうこうえん）は[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]下向山町にある県立の[[都市公園]]<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html 山梨県都市公園条例]</ref>。'''甲斐風土記の丘'''（かいふどきのおか）の愛称がある。
==概要== [[曽根丘陵]]は[[甲府盆地]]の南西縁、[[笛吹川]]左岸の[[笛吹市]]境川地域から[[市川三郷町]]にかけての広がる9キロにわたる丘陵地域で、旧石器時代からの遺跡が数多く分布している。特に甲府市南部の東山地域は弥生後期の[[方形周溝墓]]群がある[[上の平遺跡]]や、[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]や大丸山古墳を中心とする前期[[古墳]]群が密集する地域で、銚子塚古墳の史跡整備に伴い公園が建設された。園内には遺跡や古墳群のほかに[[山梨県立考古博物館]]や研修センター、広場などがある。
==施設一覧== [[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|甲斐銚子塚古墳（2010年6月撮影）]]
=== 東山古墳群 === 曽根丘陵一帯にある16基の東山古墳群のうち、園内には県内最大の[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]（[[前方後円墳]]）と[[丸山塚古墳]]（[[円墳]]）がある。どちらも[[ハイキング]]コースの一部として整備されており、登ることが可能である。 {{節スタブ}} === 山梨県立考古博物館 === '''山梨県立考古博物館'''（やまなしけんりつこうこはくぶつかん）は曽根丘陵公園内にある[[博物館]]。[[1982年]]（[[昭和]]57年）に開館<ref>{{Cite web|和書|author=山梨県教育委員会考古博物館 |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=考古博物館の紹介 |work=山梨県ホームページ |publisher=山梨県 |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref>。歴代館長に[[磯貝正義]]、[[大塚初重]]、[[渡辺誠 (建築家)|渡辺誠]]らがいる。
展示室は常設展示室・特別展示室の2つ。企画展示室はなく、年1回の秋期企画展に際しては常設展示の移動を行う。
常設展示は[[旧石器時代]]の[[ナイフ形石器]]をはじめ、[[縄文時代]]では国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている[[一の沢遺跡]]の出土品や[[殿林遺跡]]出土土器をはじめ[[有孔鍔付土器]]、[[人面装飾付土器]]、[[埋甕]]など各種土器群、[[ヒスイ]]の[[装身具]]や[[金生遺跡]]の[[配石遺構]]のジオラマなど。[[弥生時代]]では[[農耕具]]や[[弥生土器]]、[[古墳時代]]では[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]や[[大丸山古墳]]から出土した[[青銅鏡]]や[[短甲]]の[[複製品]]のほか実物資料では武具や近年出土した木製品、[[土師器]]・[[須恵器]]などが展示され、[[竪穴]]・横穴の[[石室]]構造が可動式の模型で展示されている。
そのほか、古代の[[墨書土器]]や[[甲府城]]から出土した輪宝、[[鰍沢河岸]]跡の出土品などが展示されている。
その他多目的室や学習・閲覧コーナー、[[山梨県埋蔵文化財センター]]の発掘調査報告書や研究紀要、博物館のグッズなどを販売している[[ミュージアムショップ]]が設置されている。初代館長の磯貝正義以来、[[考古学]]関係の文献を数多く収集し、「大塚文庫」として集成されている。
==== 特別展 ==== * 第一回 土偶-千の女神が語る縄文時代の祈りとくらし- * 第二回 縄文時代の酒造具-有孔鍔付土器- * 第三回 山梨の中世陶磁-やきものと甲斐人のくらし- * 第四回 古代甲斐国と畿内王権 * 第五回 古代官道と甲斐の文化 * 第六回 古代の装身具ー美と祈りの技術ー * 第七回 一粒の籾-弥生農耕の風景- * 第八回 古墳時代が聞こえる * 第九回 縄文土器 その心象世界 * 第十回 天下人の時代 * 第十一回 山梨の経塚 * 第十二回 古墳時代の甲冑 * 第十三回 黄金の都シカン発掘展 * 第十四回 ネアンデルタール人の復活展 * 第十五回 韓国・忠清北道の古代文化展 * 第十六回 遥かなるエジプト展-古代人の生活を探る- * 第十七回 縄文の旅-三内丸山から北陸、そして山梨へ- * 第十八回 中国四川省古代文物展 * 第十九回 古墳時代の金工品-黄金の輝き- * 第二十回 技と美の誕生-名宝でつづる縄文文化- * 第二十一回 遥かなるイスタンブール 大トルコ展-文明と美術- * 第二十二回 縄文の女神-人面装飾付土器の世界- * 第二十三回 縄文時代のくらし 山の民と海の民 * 第二十四回 甲府盆地から見たヤマト-甲斐銚子塚古墳の背景- * 第二十五回 世界遺産 ナスカ展 * 第二十六回 埋められた財宝ー大型装飾土器、銅鐸、そして埋蔵金ー * 第二十七回 卑弥呼時代の黄泉世界-上の平方形周溝墓群発掘30周年- * 第二十八回 発掘された女性の系譜-女性・子ども・家族- * 第二十九回 縄文土器名宝展-縄文芸術の到達点- * 第三十回 インカ帝国展-マチュピチュ「発見」100年- * 第三十一回 食いしんぼうの縄文人-いざとなったら縄文食、日本の食と心の源流- * 第三十二回 掘り起こされた音の形-まつりと音具の世界- * 第三十三回 縄文の美-世界に誇る山梨の縄文- * 第三十四回 よみがえる武士の魂-鎌倉・平泉と甲斐源氏の武家文化-<ref>『Museum Info 2016』</ref> * 第三十五回 ひつぎのヒミツ-棺から読み解く古墳時代-
=== 研修センター === '''風土記の丘研修センター'''（ふどきのおかけんしゅうせんたー）は曽根丘陵公園内にある山梨県立の研修所。[[1989年]]開館。
[[縄文土器]]作りや石器時代の火起こし体験などが体験できるほか、各種講演会場としても使用されている。 {{節スタブ}} === その他 === 園内には展望広場や[[バーベキュー|バーベキュー場]]、[[日本庭園]]、[[テニスコート]]などが設置されている。
==関連項目== *[[日本の歴史公園100選]]
==交通アクセス== *[[甲府駅]]（[[甲府駅バスターミナル]]）3番のりばより **[[山梨交通]]75系統「豊富」行きに乗車、「県立考古博物館」または「銚子塚古墳入口」下車。 *[[新宿駅]]（[[バスタ新宿]]）より **[[中央高速バス]] 新宿 - 甲府線(甲府南経由)に乗車、「中道」下車徒歩5分。 *[[中央自動車道]][[甲府南インターチェンジ]]より車すぐ。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references/>
==外部リンク== {{commonscat|Sone kyuryo park}} *[http://www.sonekyuryo.jp 曽根丘陵公園] *[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html 山梨県立考古博物館]
{{山梨県施設一覧}} {{DEFAULTSORT:そねきゆうりようこうえん}} [[Category:山梨県の公園]] [[Category:甲府市の地理]]

{{park |name = Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Yamanashi Prefecture Sone Kyuryo Park''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ}}.
== References ==

== External Links ==

'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).

The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|12=draft|12=draft}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|12=draft|12=draft}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|12=draft|12=draft}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|12=draft|12=draft}}. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.

The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|12=draft|12=draft}}.

The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|12=draft|12=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|12=draft|12=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=draft|12=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|12=draft|12=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|12=draft|12=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}}.

{{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Parks in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu, Yamanashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kai Fudoki Hill]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fudoki Hills]] }}
[[sn:Kai Fudoki Hill]] [[zh:山梨県曽根丘陵公園]] [[ja:%E6%9B%BD%E6%A0%B9%E4%B8%98%E9%99%B5%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92]] [[sn:ai Fudoki Hill]] [[en:Kai Fudoki Hill]]
<!--Kai Fudoki Hill-->

{{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|qq=draft|qq=draft}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki River|qq=}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki|qq=}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikawamisato|qq=}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Knife-shaped stone tools|qq=}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Properties|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Short armor|13=User:Immanuelle/Tankō|qq=}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/barbecue|qq=}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese garden|qq=}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tennis courts|qq=}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Station|qq=}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Kotsu|qq=}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjuku Station|qq=}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chuo Expressway Bus|qq=}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chuo Expressway|qq=}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.

The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki River|qq=}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki|qq=}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikawamisato|qq=}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.

The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Knife-shaped stone tools|qq=}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Properties|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Short armor|13=User:Immanuelle/Tankō|qq=}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/barbecue|qq=}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese garden|qq=}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tennis courts|qq=}}.

The {{ill|Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki River|qq=}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki|qq=}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikawamisato|qq=}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Knife-shaped stone tools|qq=}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Properties|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Short armor|13=User:Immanuelle/Tankō|qq=}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/barbecue|qq=}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese garden|qq=}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tennis courts|qq=}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Station|qq=}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Kotsu|qq=}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjuku Station|qq=}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chuo Expressway Bus|qq=}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chuo Expressway|qq=}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.

{{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|qq=}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|qq=}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).

The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}, training centers, and open plazas.

'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki River|qq=}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki|qq=}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikawamisato|qq=}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Knife-shaped stone tools|qq=}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Properties|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Short armor|13=User:Immanuelle/Tankō|qq=}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/barbecue|qq=}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese garden|qq=}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tennis courts|qq=}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Station|qq=}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Kotsu|qq=}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjuku Station|qq=}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chuo Expressway Bus|qq=}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chuo Expressway|qq=}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.

{{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|qq=}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}} Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|qq=}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}.<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the {{ill|Fuefuki River|ja|笛吹川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki River|qq=}}'s left bank in {{ill|Fuefuki|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki|qq=}}'s Sakagawa region to {{ill|Ichikawamisato|ja|市川三郷町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikawamisato|qq=}}. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=}} and early {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} clusters centered around the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|{{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the {{ill|Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|lt=Kai Choshizuka Kofun|ja|甲斐銚子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Choshizuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as {{ill|Knife-shaped stone tools|ja|ナイフ形石器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Knife-shaped stone tools|qq=}}, Jomon period artifacts designated as {{ill|Important Cultural Properties|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Properties|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=}} and {{ill|Short armor|ja|短甲|1=Tankō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Short armor|13=User:Immanuelle/Tankō|qq=}} from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, {{ill|barbecue|ja|バーベキュー|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/barbecue|qq=}} areas, a {{ill|Japanese garden|ja|日本庭園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese garden|qq=}}, and {{ill|tennis courts|ja|テニスコート|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tennis courts|qq=}}.
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=}}
== Access ==
* From {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Station|qq=}} (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the {{ill|Yamanashi Kotsu|ja|山梨交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Kotsu|qq=}} Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From {{ill|Shinjuku Station|ja|新宿駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinjuku Station|qq=}} (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the {{ill|Chuo Expressway Bus|ja|中央高速バス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chuo Expressway Bus|qq=}} bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the {{ill|Chuo Expressway|ja|中央自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chuo Expressway|qq=}} {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.

'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=}}
== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.

== External Links ==
* [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Sone Kyuryo Park] * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum]

revision.text:
{{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|qq=}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|qq=}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==

Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.
