'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=}}
== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Sone Kyuryo Park] * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum]

<!--Kai Fudoki Hill--> [[Category:Parks and gardens in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu, Yamanashi]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Sone kyuryo park}} [[ja:曽根丘陵公園]]

{{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|qq=}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=}}
== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==

The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=}}
== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Fixed Kofun templates
revision.text:
{{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|qq=}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.

The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].

* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.

'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=}}
== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Sone Kyuryo Park] * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum]

Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|qq=}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=}}
== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Sone Kyuryo Park] * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum]

[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=}}
== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.

== External Links ==
* [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Sone Kyuryo Park] * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|qq=}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].

== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11515320]] Wikidata name: Sone Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|qq=}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=}}
== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=}}.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links ==
* [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Sone Kyuryo Park] * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/index.html Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum]

[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503167}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=|1=Masayoshi Isogai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588043}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=|1=Makoto Watanabe (architect)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16768581}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=|1=Ichinosawa Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352271}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡|1=bronze mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1041984}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}
== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=|1=Kofu-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577782}}.

<!--Kai Fudoki Hill--> [[Category:Parks and gardens in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu, Yamanashi]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Sone kyuryo park}} [[ja:曽根丘陵公園]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11515320]] Wikidata name: Sone Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|qq=|1=designated administrator|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497972}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503167}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=|1=Masayoshi Isogai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588043}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=|1=Makoto Watanabe (architect)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16768581}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=|1=Ichinosawa Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352271}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡|1=bronze mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1041984}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].

Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=|1=Kofu-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577782}}.

'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503167}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=|1=Masayoshi Isogai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588043}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=|1=Makoto Watanabe (architect)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16768581}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=|1=Ichinosawa Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352271}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡|1=bronze mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1041984}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.

The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].

== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=|1=Kofu-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577782}}.
