[[zh:山梨県曽根丘陵公園]]
<!--Kai Fudoki Hill--> [[Category:Parks and gardens in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu, Yamanashi]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Sone kyuryo park}} [[ja:曽根丘陵公園]] {{wikidata link|Q11515320}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11515320]] Wikidata name: Sone Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|qq=|1=designated administrator|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497972}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503167}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=|1=Masayoshi Isogai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588043}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=|1=Makoto Watanabe (architect)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16768581}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=|1=Ichinosawa Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352271}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡|1=bronze mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1041984}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].

== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=|1=Kofu-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577782}}.

[[zh:山梨県曽根丘陵公園]]
<!--Kai Fudoki Hill--> [[Category:Parks and gardens in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu, Yamanashi]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Sone kyuryo park}} [[ja:曽根丘陵公園]] {{wikidata link|Q11515320}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11515320]] Wikidata name: Sone Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |classification = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |location = [[Kofu City]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Shimomukaiyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opening = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|designated manager|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/designated manager|qq=|1=designated administrator|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497972}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of designated management facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office_address = Shimomukaiyamamachi 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official website] }} '''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園, やまなしけんそねきゅうりょうこうえん) is a prefectural [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu City]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview == The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, spanning from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]] left bank in the [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] Sakagawa area to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato Town]]. The region contains numerous archaeological sites dating back to the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic]]. The eastern Yamato area in southern Kofu is particularly notable for late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] {{ill|square moated tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square moated tombs|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} at the Uenodaira site and an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] cluster of Kofuns, including the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was constructed as part of the historical preservation of Choshizuka Kofun and includes archaeological sites, Kofuns, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=曽根丘陵公園}}, a training center, and open spaces.
== Facilities == [[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|Kai Choshizuka Kofun (photographed June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster === Among the 16 Kofuns in the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster, the park contains the prefecture's largest [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (a keyhole-shaped mound) and the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun|qq=|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503167}} (a round mound). Both are integrated into hiking trails and accessible for climbing. {{Stub section}}
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum === The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館, やまなしけんりつこうこはくぶつかん) opened in 1982.{{sfn|Yamanashi Prefecture Education Board|2015}} Notable directors have included {{ill|Isogai Masayoshi|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isogai Masayoshi|qq=|1=Masayoshi Isogai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588043}}, {{ill|Otsuka Hatsushige|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuka Hatsushige|qq=|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}}, and [[:en:Makoto Sei Watanabe|Watanabe Makoto (architect)]].
The museum features two exhibit rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room; the permanent displays are rearranged for the annual autumn exhibition.
Permanent exhibits include: * [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic]] knife-shaped stone tools. * Artifacts from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]], including nationally designated [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], such as items from the {{ill|Ichinosawa Site|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Site|qq=|1=Ichinosawa Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352271}}, and a variety of pottery, including {{ill|pierced-lugged pottery|ja|有孔鍔付土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pierced-lugged pottery|qq=|1=yūkō tsubatsuki pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516351}} and {{ill|human-faced pottery|ja|人面装飾付土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-faced pottery|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Dioramas of the [[:en:Kinsei ruins|Kinjo Site]]. * [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] farming tools and pottery. * Exhibits from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], such as replicas of bronze mirrors and armor excavated from Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, as well as artifacts like weapons, wooden objects, and ceramics.
Additional features include multipurpose rooms, learning and reading areas, a museum shop selling excavation reports and research journals from the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and archaeology-related merchandise. Many archaeological texts are collected under the "Otsuka Library."
==== Special Exhibitions ==== Special exhibitions range from Jomon artifacts to ancient international cultural exchanges. Highlights include: * "Jomon Pottery: A World of Imagery" * "Ancient Kofun Armor" * "Golden Treasures of the Sican" * "Life in Ancient Egypt" * "The Inca Empire: 100 Years of Machu Picchu Discovery"
=== Training Center === The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) opened in 1989. It offers hands-on experiences, such as crafting Jomon pottery and starting fires with stone tools, and serves as a venue for lectures. {{Stub section}}
=== Other Features === The park also includes viewing platforms, a [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] area, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}
== Access == * From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Bus Terminal, Platform 3): ** Take Yamanashi Kotsu Bus Line 75 bound for "Toyotomi," alight at "Prefectural Archaeological Museum" or "Choshizuka Kofun Entrance." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the Chuo Expressway Bus (Shinjuku-Kofu Line via Kofu-Minami), alight at "Nakamichi" (5-minute walk). * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] Kofu-Minami Interchange.

'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503167}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=|1=Masayoshi Isogai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588043}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=|1=Makoto Watanabe (architect)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16768581}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=|1=Ichinosawa Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352271}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡|1=bronze mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1041984}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}
== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=|1=Kofu-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577782}}.

[[zh:山梨県曽根丘陵公園]]
<!--Kai Fudoki Hill--> [[Category:Parks and gardens in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu, Yamanashi]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Sone kyuryo park}} [[ja:曽根丘陵公園]] {{wikidata link|Q11515320}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11515320]] Wikidata name: Sone Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |classification = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |location = [[Kofu City]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Shimomukaiyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opening = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|designated manager|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/designated manager|qq=|1=designated administrator|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497972}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of designated management facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office_address = Shimomukaiyamamachi 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official website] }} '''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園, やまなしけんそねきゅうりょうこうえん) is a prefectural [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu City]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview == The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, spanning from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]] left bank in the [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] Sakagawa area to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato Town]]. The region contains numerous archaeological sites dating back to the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic]]. The eastern Yamato area in southern Kofu is particularly notable for late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] {{ill|square moated tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square moated tombs|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} at the Uenodaira site and an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] cluster of Kofuns, including the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was constructed as part of the historical preservation of Choshizuka Kofun and includes archaeological sites, Kofuns, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=曽根丘陵公園}}, a training center, and open spaces.
== Facilities == [[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|Kai Choshizuka Kofun (photographed June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster === Among the 16 Kofuns in the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster, the park contains the prefecture's largest [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (a keyhole-shaped mound) and the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun|qq=|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503167}} (a round mound). Both are integrated into hiking trails and accessible for climbing. {{Stub section}}
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum === The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館, やまなしけんりつこうこはくぶつかん) opened in 1982.{{sfn|Yamanashi Prefecture Education Board|2015}} Notable directors have included {{ill|Isogai Masayoshi|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isogai Masayoshi|qq=|1=Masayoshi Isogai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588043}}, {{ill|Otsuka Hatsushige|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuka Hatsushige|qq=|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}}, and [[:en:Makoto Sei Watanabe|Watanabe Makoto (architect)]].
The museum features two exhibit rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room; the permanent displays are rearranged for the annual autumn exhibition.
Permanent exhibits include: * [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic]] knife-shaped stone tools. * Artifacts from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]], including nationally designated [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], such as items from the {{ill|Ichinosawa Site|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Site|qq=|1=Ichinosawa Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352271}}, and a variety of pottery, including {{ill|pierced-lugged pottery|ja|有孔鍔付土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pierced-lugged pottery|qq=|1=yūkō tsubatsuki pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516351}} and {{ill|human-faced pottery|ja|人面装飾付土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-faced pottery|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Dioramas of the [[:en:Kinsei ruins|Kinjo Site]]. * [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] farming tools and pottery. * Exhibits from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], such as replicas of bronze mirrors and armor excavated from Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, as well as artifacts like weapons, wooden objects, and ceramics.
Additional features include multipurpose rooms, learning and reading areas, a museum shop selling excavation reports and research journals from the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and archaeology-related merchandise. Many archaeological texts are collected under the "Otsuka Library."
==== Special Exhibitions ==== Special exhibitions range from Jomon artifacts to ancient international cultural exchanges. Highlights include: * "Jomon Pottery: A World of Imagery" * "Ancient Kofun Armor" * "Golden Treasures of the Sican" * "Life in Ancient Egypt" * "The Inca Empire: 100 Years of Machu Picchu Discovery"
=== Training Center === The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) opened in 1989. It offers hands-on experiences, such as crafting Jomon pottery and starting fires with stone tools, and serves as a venue for lectures. {{Stub section}}
=== Other Features === The park also includes viewing platforms, a [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] area, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}
== Access == * From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Bus Terminal, Platform 3): ** Take Yamanashi Kotsu Bus Line 75 bound for "Toyotomi," alight at "Prefectural Archaeological Museum" or "Choshizuka Kofun Entrance." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the Chuo Expressway Bus (Shinjuku-Kofu Line via Kofu-Minami), alight at "Nakamichi" (5-minute walk). * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] Kofu-Minami Interchange.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sone kyuryo park]][[ja:曽根丘陵公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11515320]] Wikidata name: Sone Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|qq=|1=designated administrator|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497972}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503167}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.

It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=|1=Masayoshi Isogai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588043}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=|1=Makoto Watanabe (architect)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16768581}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=|1=Ichinosawa Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352271}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡|1=bronze mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1041984}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.

=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}
== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=|1=Kofu-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577782}}.

[[zh:山梨県曽根丘陵公園]]
<!--Kai Fudoki Hill--> [[Category:Parks and gardens in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu, Yamanashi]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Sone kyuryo park}} [[ja:曽根丘陵公園]] {{wikidata link|Q11515320}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11515320]] Wikidata name: Sone Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |classification = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |location = [[Kofu City]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Shimomukaiyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opening = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|designated manager|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/designated manager|qq=|1=designated administrator|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497972}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of designated management facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office_address = Shimomukaiyamamachi 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official website] }} '''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園, やまなしけんそねきゅうりょうこうえん) is a prefectural [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu City]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview == The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, spanning from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]] left bank in the [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] Sakagawa area to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato Town]]. The region contains numerous archaeological sites dating back to the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic]]. The eastern Yamato area in southern Kofu is particularly notable for late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] {{ill|square moated tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square moated tombs|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} at the Uenodaira site and an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] cluster of Kofuns, including the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was constructed as part of the historical preservation of Choshizuka Kofun and includes archaeological sites, Kofuns, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=曽根丘陵公園}}, a training center, and open spaces.
== Facilities == [[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|Kai Choshizuka Kofun (photographed June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster === Among the 16 Kofuns in the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster, the park contains the prefecture's largest [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (a keyhole-shaped mound) and the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun|qq=|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503167}} (a round mound). Both are integrated into hiking trails and accessible for climbing. {{Stub section}}
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum === The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館, やまなしけんりつこうこはくぶつかん) opened in 1982.{{sfn|Yamanashi Prefecture Education Board|2015}} Notable directors have included {{ill|Isogai Masayoshi|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isogai Masayoshi|qq=|1=Masayoshi Isogai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588043}}, {{ill|Otsuka Hatsushige|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuka Hatsushige|qq=|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}}, and [[:en:Makoto Sei Watanabe|Watanabe Makoto (architect)]].
The museum features two exhibit rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room; the permanent displays are rearranged for the annual autumn exhibition.
Permanent exhibits include: * [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic]] knife-shaped stone tools. * Artifacts from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]], including nationally designated [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], such as items from the {{ill|Ichinosawa Site|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Site|qq=|1=Ichinosawa Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352271}}, and a variety of pottery, including {{ill|pierced-lugged pottery|ja|有孔鍔付土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pierced-lugged pottery|qq=|1=yūkō tsubatsuki pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516351}} and {{ill|human-faced pottery|ja|人面装飾付土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-faced pottery|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Dioramas of the [[:en:Kinsei ruins|Kinjo Site]]. * [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] farming tools and pottery. * Exhibits from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], such as replicas of bronze mirrors and armor excavated from Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, as well as artifacts like weapons, wooden objects, and ceramics.
Additional features include multipurpose rooms, learning and reading areas, a museum shop selling excavation reports and research journals from the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and archaeology-related merchandise. Many archaeological texts are collected under the "Otsuka Library."
==== Special Exhibitions ==== Special exhibitions range from Jomon artifacts to ancient international cultural exchanges. Highlights include: * "Jomon Pottery: A World of Imagery" * "Ancient Kofun Armor" * "Golden Treasures of the Sican" * "Life in Ancient Egypt" * "The Inca Empire: 100 Years of Machu Picchu Discovery"
=== Training Center === The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) opened in 1989. It offers hands-on experiences, such as crafting Jomon pottery and starting fires with stone tools, and serves as a venue for lectures. {{Stub section}}
=== Other Features === The park also includes viewing platforms, a [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] area, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}
== Access == * From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Bus Terminal, Platform 3): ** Take Yamanashi Kotsu Bus Line 75 bound for "Toyotomi," alight at "Prefectural Archaeological Museum" or "Choshizuka Kofun Entrance." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the Chuo Expressway Bus (Shinjuku-Kofu Line via Kofu-Minami), alight at "Nakamichi" (5-minute walk). * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] Kofu-Minami Interchange.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sone kyuryo park]][[ja:曽根丘陵公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11515320]] Wikidata name: Sone Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|qq=|1=designated administrator|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497972}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|qq=|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503167}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|qq=|1=Masayoshi Isogai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588043}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|qq=|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|qq=|1=Makoto Watanabe (architect)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16768581}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|qq=|1=Ichinosawa Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352271}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡|1=bronze mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1041984}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].

== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|qq=|1=Kofu-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577782}}.

It is affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview == The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, spanning from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]] left bank in the [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] Sakagawa area to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato Town]]. The region contains numerous archaeological sites dating back to the [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic]]. The eastern Yamato area in southern Kofu is particularly notable for late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] {{ill|square moated tombs|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square moated tombs|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} at the Uenodaira site and an early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] cluster of Kofuns, including the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was constructed as part of the historical preservation of Choshizuka Kofun and includes archaeological sites, Kofuns, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=曽根丘陵公園}}, a training center, and open spaces.
== Facilities == [[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|Kai Choshizuka Kofun (photographed June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster === Among the 16 Kofuns in the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster, the park contains the prefecture's largest [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (a keyhole-shaped mound) and the {{ill|Maruyamazuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyamazuka Kofun|qq=|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503167}} (a round mound). Both are integrated into hiking trails and accessible for climbing. {{Stub section}}
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum === The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館, やまなしけんりつこうこはくぶつかん) opened in 1982.{{sfn|Yamanashi Prefecture Education Board|2015}} Notable directors have included {{ill|Isogai Masayoshi|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isogai Masayoshi|qq=|1=Masayoshi Isogai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588043}}, {{ill|Otsuka Hatsushige|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuka Hatsushige|qq=|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}}, and [[:en:Makoto Sei Watanabe|Watanabe Makoto (architect)]].
The museum features two exhibit rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room; the permanent displays are rearranged for the annual autumn exhibition.
Permanent exhibits include: * [[:en:Paleolithic|Paleolithic]] knife-shaped stone tools. * Artifacts from the [[:en:Jōmon period|Jomon period]], including nationally designated [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], such as items from the {{ill|Ichinosawa Site|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Site|qq=|1=Ichinosawa Site|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352271}}, and a variety of pottery, including {{ill|pierced-lugged pottery|ja|有孔鍔付土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pierced-lugged pottery|qq=|1=yūkō tsubatsuki pottery|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11516351}} and {{ill|human-faced pottery|ja|人面装飾付土器|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/human-faced pottery|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Dioramas of the [[:en:Kinsei ruins|Kinjo Site]]. * [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] farming tools and pottery. * Exhibits from the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], such as replicas of bronze mirrors and armor excavated from Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, as well as artifacts like weapons, wooden objects, and ceramics.
Additional features include multipurpose rooms, learning and reading areas, a museum shop selling excavation reports and research journals from the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center|ja|山梨県埋蔵文化財センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and archaeology-related merchandise. Many archaeological texts are collected under the "Otsuka Library."
==== Special Exhibitions ==== Special exhibitions range from Jomon artifacts to ancient international cultural exchanges. Highlights include: * "Jomon Pottery: A World of Imagery" * "Ancient Kofun Armor" * "Golden Treasures of the Sican" * "Life in Ancient Egypt" * "The Inca Empire: 100 Years of Machu Picchu Discovery"
=== Training Center === The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) opened in 1989. It offers hands-on experiences, such as crafting Jomon pottery and starting fires with stone tools, and serves as a venue for lectures. {{Stub section}}
=== Other Features === The park also includes viewing platforms, a [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] area, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|qq=|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}
== Access == * From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Bus Terminal, Platform 3): ** Take Yamanashi Kotsu Bus Line 75 bound for "Toyotomi," alight at "Prefectural Archaeological Museum" or "Choshizuka Kofun Entrance." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the Chuo Expressway Bus (Shinjuku-Kofu Line via Kofu-Minami), alight at "Nakamichi" (5-minute walk). * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] Kofu-Minami Interchange.

[[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
<!-- no translated page template found in history --> [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|曽根丘陵公園|version=98102595|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Parks and gardens in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu, Yamanashi]] {{wikidata link|Q11515320}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sone kyuryo park]][[ja:曽根丘陵公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11515320]] Wikidata name: Sone Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|1=designated administrator|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497972}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503167}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|1=Masayoshi Isogai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588043}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|1=Makoto Watanabe (architect)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16768581}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|1=Ichinosawa Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352271}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡|1=bronze mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1041984}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}
== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|1=Kofu-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577782}}.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11515320]] Wikidata name: Sone Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |classification = [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] (wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |location = [[Kofu City]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Shimomukaiyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opening = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|designated manager|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/designated manager|1=designated administrator|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497972}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of designated management facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office_address = Shimomukaiyamamachi 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official website] }} '''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園, やまなしけんそねきゅうりょうこうえん) is a prefectural [[:en:Urban park|Urban park]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu City]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]. It is affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview == The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, spanning from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]] left bank in the [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki City]] Sakagawa area to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato Town]]. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Sone kyuryo park]][[ja:曽根丘陵公園]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11515320]] Wikidata name: Sone Kyūryō Park [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{park |name = Kai Fudoki Hill |image = [[File:甲斐風土記の丘の遊具 - panoramio.jpg|300px]] |type = {{ill|Urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Urban park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} (Wide-area park) |area = 40.4 ha |city = [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]][[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] Shimomukouyama 1271 |coordinates = {{coord|35|35|32|N|138|34|57|E|region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |opened = [[1986]] |operator = Yamanashi Prefecture ({{ill|Designated administrator|ja|指定管理者|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Designated administrator|1=designated administrator|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11497972}}: Fuji Kanko Kaihatsu & Fuji Greentech)<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/gyoukaku/shiteikanri/ichiran.html List of Designated Administrator Facilities]</ref> |office = Sone Kyuryo Park Management Office |office address = Shimomukouyama Town 1271, Kofu City, Yamanashi Prefecture |official website = [http://www.sonekyuryo.jp Official Homepage] }}
'''Kai Fudoki Hill''' (山梨県曽根丘陵公園) is a prefectural {{ill|urban park|ja|都市公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/urban park|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公園|1=park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22698}} located in Shimomukouyama Town, [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]].<ref>[http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/somu/shigaku/reiki/reiki_honbun/aa50009561.html Yamanashi Prefecture Urban Park Ordinance]</ref> It is also affectionately known as '''Kai Fudoki no Oka''' (甲斐風土記の丘).
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Hill Range|lt=Sone Kyuryo|ja|曽根丘陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sone Kyuryo|qq=}} extends for about 9 kilometers along the southwestern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}, from the [[:en:Fuefuki River|Fuefuki River]]'s left bank in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki]]'s Sakagawa region to [[:en:Ichikawamisato|Ichikawamisato]]. The area is rich with archaeological sites dating back to the Paleolithic period. In particular, the eastern Yamayama region in southern Kofu is densely packed with late Yayoi period {{ill|Square moated tomb|ja|方形周溝墓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square moated tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弥生時代の墓制|1=Funerary practices of the Yayoi period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487466}} clusters such as the {{ill|Ueno Taira Ruins|ja|上の平遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ueno Taira Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and early [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] clusters centered around the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] and Omariyama Kofun. The park was established alongside the preservation of the Choshizuka Kofun historic site. Within the park are ancient ruins, kofun clusters, the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}, training centers, and open plazas.
== List of Facilities ==
[[File:Kai-Choshizuka-kofun №1.JPG|thumb|240px|[[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] (Photo taken June 2010)]]
=== Higashiyama Kofun Cluster ===
Among the 16 kofun mounds of the Higashiyama Kofun Cluster spread across the Sone Kyuryo, the park features Yamanashi Prefecture's largest kofun, the [[:en:Kai Chōshizuka Kofun|Kai Choshizuka Kofun]] ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]), and the {{ill|Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|ja|丸山塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruyama Tsuka Kofun|1=Maruyamazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17503167}} ({{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). Both are part of hiking trails and are accessible to climb.
=== Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum ===
The '''Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum''' (山梨県立考古博物館) is situated within Sone Kyuryo Park. It opened in [[:en:1982|1982]] (Showa 57).<ref>{{Cite web|author=Yamanashi Prefectural Board of Education Archaeological Museum |date=2015-01-30 |url=http://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/kouko-hak/infomation/koukohakubutukan-shoukai.html|title=Introduction to the Archaeological Museum |publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture |accessdate=2015-08-21}}</ref> Notable past directors include {{ill|Masayoshi Isogai|ja|磯貝正義|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masayoshi Isogai|1=Masayoshi Isogai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588043}}, {{ill|Hatsushige Otsuka|ja|大塚初重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hatsushige Otsuka|1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433969}}, and {{ill|Makoto Watanabe (architect)|ja|渡辺誠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Makoto Watanabe (architect)|1=Makoto Watanabe (architect)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16768581}}.
The museum features two exhibition rooms: a permanent exhibition room and a special exhibition room. There is no dedicated planning exhibition room, but the permanent exhibition is rearranged annually for the autumn special exhibition.
The permanent exhibit showcases artifacts from the Paleolithic period such as [[:en:Blade (archaeology)|Knife-shaped stone tools]], Jomon period artifacts designated as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] from sites like {{ill|Ichinosawa Ruins|ja|一の沢遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichinosawa Ruins|1=Ichinosawa Ruins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11352271}}, and various Jomon pottery types. The Yayoi period section includes agricultural tools and pottery, while the Kofun period section features replicas of {{ill|Bronze mirrors|ja|青銅鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bronze mirrors|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 銅鏡|1=bronze mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1041984}} and [[:en:Tankō|Tankō]] from the Kai Choshizuka Kofun and Omariyama Kofun, along with actual artifacts such as weaponry and recent wooden findings.
==== Special Exhibitions ==== * 1st: "Dogū - Prayers and Life in the Jomon Era" * 2nd: "Brewing Tools of the Jomon Era" * ... (continue as listed in the original document)
=== Training Center ===
The '''Fudoki no Oka Training Center''' (風土記の丘研修センター) is a prefectural training facility within the park, opened in [[:en:1989|1989]]. Visitors can experience activities like Jomon pottery making and stone age fire-starting, and the center also hosts lectures and events.
=== Other Facilities ===
The park includes an observation plaza, [[:en:Barbecue|barbecue]] areas, a [[:en:Japanese garden|Japanese garden]], and [[:en:Tennis court|tennis courts]].
== Related Pages ==
* {{ill|100 Historical Parks of Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Parks of Japan|1=100 Best Historical Parks in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505987}}
== Access ==
* From [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] (Kofu Bus Terminal), platform 3: ** Take the [[:en:Yamanashi Kōtsū|Yamanashi Kotsu]] Route 75 bus bound for Toyotomi. Get off at "Kenritsu Koko Hakubutsukan" or "Choshizuka Kofun Iriguchi." * From [[:en:Shinjuku Station|Shinjuku Station]] (Busta Shinjuku): ** Take the [[:en:Chūō Kōsoku Bus|Chuo Expressway Bus]] bound for Kofu via Kofu Minami. Get off at "Nakado," then walk 5 minutes. * By car: Immediately accessible from the [[:en:Chūō Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] {{ill|Kofu Minami Interchange|ja|甲府南インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Minami Interchange|1=Kofu-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577782}}.
